codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of box shadow rendering. Shadow fields added to Node with correct defaults, compile-time size assertion updated, has_shadow() guard prevents unnecessary work. Shadow rendering supports hard shadows (blur=0) and Gaussian-blurred shadows via the drawing library. Group opacity path correctly sizes offscreen buffers to include shadow overflow. Damage tracking in abs_bounds correctly expands dirty rects. 4 MiB allocation cap prevents OOM. Both core and compositor scene_state.rs updated consistently. N_SHADOW node retained as structural placeholder with zero height.

issues.description: Hard shadow path casts sx/sy to u32 (`sx as u32`) without clamping. If the shadow extends past the left/top edge of the framebuffer (sx < 0), the u32 wrap causes fill_rect_blend to skip the entire shadow rather than partially clip it. The blurred shadow path correctly uses `.max(0)` (line 425). Harmless in practice since nodes are positioned with positive coordinates, but inconsistent between the two paths.

Blurred shadow blit uses `(sx - pad as i32).max(0) as u32` which prevents negative coordinates, but doesn't adjust the source offset into the blur buffer. If the shadow is partially off-screen to the left/top, the blit starts at x=0 but reads from the beginning of the blur buffer, causing a slight positional shift. Only affects shadows at the very edge of the framebuffer.

abs_bounds shadow overflow uses logical coordinates (no scale factor) while shadow_overflow in scene_render.rs applies scale. This is correct for their respective purposes (abs_bounds feeds diff_scenes which operates in logical space), but the parallel implementations with subtly different semantics could diverge in future maintenance. A comment noting the intentional difference would help.
Hard shadow path casts potentially-negative sx/sy to u32 without clamping, relying on drawing library bounds checking to skip the draw. The blurred path handles this correctly with .max(0).

Parallel shadow overflow computations in abs_bounds (logical, no scale) and shadow_overflow (physical, with scale) work correctly but could benefit from a cross-reference comment to prevent future drift.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md documents that Node changes require updating the compile-time size assertion and both core/compositor agreement. The worker followed this convention correctly (size 60→72, both scene_state.rs files updated). No gap here — convention was followed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: system/libraries/scene/lib.rs size assertion updated; both system/services/core/scene_state.rs and system/services/compositor/scene_state.rs have identical shadow field initialization.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shadow rendering pattern (allocate offscreen buffer, fill shape, blur, composite) is now a reusable pattern in the codebase. Library knowledge file architecture.md should note that shadow rendering lives in scene_render.rs::render_shadow and the 4 MiB cap per shadow buffer, since future features (e.g., text shadows, inset shadows) will follow the same pattern.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: system/services/compositor/scene_render.rs lines 306-435: render_shadow() function with 4 MiB allocation cap and fallback to hard shadow.

gaps: No test for negative shadow_spread (shrinking shadow footprint)
No test for shadow with corner_radius > 0 (rounded shadow shape)
No test for shadow at framebuffer edge (negative coordinate clipping)

summary: Box shadow rendering implementation is correct and well-tested. All 9 validation assertions (VAL-BLUR-008/009/010/011/012/015, VAL-CROSS-004/006/011) are covered by targeted tests. The code handles both hard and blurred shadows, respects group opacity, scales correctly, and expands damage rects for shadow overflow. Two non-blocking edge case issues identified: hard shadow negative coordinate casts and blurred shadow blit offset at framebuffer edges. 10 new tests added, all passing.
